name: Dino Facts
white: A T. rex trying its absolute hardest to clap.
Tiny arms and enormous feelings.
A velociraptor who learned to open doors and immediately chose chaos.
A stegosaurus backing up without checking its mirrors.
Two paleontologists screaming at each other about feathers.
A triceratops stuck in the doggy door.
An asteroid with absolutely terrible timing.
A brachiosaurus with a crick in its neck.
Brushing dirt off the same bone for eleven years.
The gift shop's plush T. rex with regular-sized arms.
A pterodactyl screech instead of an alarm clock.
Birds, which are just tiny surviving dinosaurs with attitude.
A 66-million-year-old fossilized poop in a glass display case.
A dinosaur burp that flattens the ferns.
A dinosaur theme park with very poor fencing decisions.
A dinosaur the size of a chicken with the confidence of a much bigger dinosaur.
An ankylosaurus ringing the doorbell with its tail club.
A herd of long-necks blocking traffic since Tuesday.
A baby dinosaur imprinting on a garbage truck.
Amber with a very smug mosquito inside.
A museum skeleton assembled with the head on backwards.
Seventy tons of dinosaur tiptoeing past a sleeping baby.
A tail swipe that clears the entire snack table.
A T. rex playing hide-and-seek behind one small fern.
Dino nuggets shaped like no dinosaur known to science.
The world's first flying lesson, off the world's tallest cliff.
A dinosaur trying to blow out birthday candles without eating the cake.
An egg with something enormous tapping from the inside.
Paleontologists high-fiving over a single toe bone.
A dinosaur roar with a little voice crack in it.

black.text: Paleontologists have finally figured out what T. rex used its tiny arms for: _.
Museum rules: no flash photography, no touching the bones, and absolutely no _.
Deep inside a chunk of amber, scientists found a perfectly preserved _.
Attention theme park guests: the fences are down, and the raptors have discovered _.
The dinosaur's last words before the asteroid hit: "_."
Sixty-six million years later, the fossil record clearly shows _.
A brachiosaurus's neck is long enough to reach exactly one thing: _.
The velociraptor opened the door, took one look at _, and walked off with _.
